findings.msg: 'What principle should guide picking the best multiple-choice answer? Occam's razor' is exam-taking strategy, not genetics content.
findings.fix: Cut it, or rewrite as a genetics-reasoning card (e.g. ss vs ds inference from Chargaff).

findings.msg: Tagged `telomere` but the card is entirely about prokaryotic (circular-chromosome) replication, which has no telomeres and none are discussed.
findings.fix: Drop `telomere`; use `replication-origin` and/or `leading-lagging`.

findings.msg: L1-R045 and L1-C017 are the same card — 'name the three DNA-hereditary experiments with dates/scientists' (Griffith 1928 / Avery 1944 / Hershey-Chase 1952).
findings.fix: Delete one; keep the concept version (L1-C017) since it adds 'what each contributed.'

findings.msg: L1-C029 and L1-C018 both ask 'what setup let Avery test which molecule + who developed it' → Martin Dawson's in vitro transformation, near-verbatim.
findings.fix: Merge into one card.

findings.msg: L1 (intro: history + DNA structure + techniques) holds ~100 of 459 cards (~22%), inflated by a second pass that re-created Griffith/Avery/Hershey-Chase/blotting/gel cards; disproportionate to its typical exam weight vs denser L6/L7 regulation material.
findings.fix: Prune ~15-20 redundant L1 recall/concept cards to rebalance toward L6-L7.

findings.msg: Says the −35 and −10 elements are 'separated by ~25 bp (variable spacer)'; the conserved spacer is ~17 bp (16–19), and card L3-P002 correctly uses 17 bp — internal contradiction.
findings.fix: Change '~25 bp' to '~17 bp (16–19 bp variable spacer).'

findings.msg: Dates the telomerase discovery to 1987; Greider & Blackburn discovered telomerase in 1984 (published Cell 1985).
findings.fix: Change '(1987)' to '(1985)' for the telomerase discovery.

findings.msg: Pervasive instructor-tell annotations ('she flagged this as exam-important', 'she said this is likely on the exam', 'her single loudest exam signal in purple') across L2/L3/L4 reveal cards built from one instructor's verbal exam hints rather than the curriculum.
findings.fix: Strip the 'she said/exam-important' meta and keep the underlying concept, so cards teach the material not the test.

findings.msg: Many problem cards embed the specific past-exam answer letter ('Confirmed in lecture — answer C', 'answer B'), tying the card to one exam's key instead of the concept.
findings.fix: Remove the '(Confirmed — answer X)' tags; the reasoning already stands alone.

findings.msg: Card explicitly resolves 'HW1 Q6' and cross-references L1-P012, anchoring content to a specific past homework rather than the general concept.
findings.fix: Drop the HW1/cross-ref parenthetical.

findings.msg: 'Archbold told you to cross out the telomere figure in the textbook' defines the card around one lecture's slide correction, which won't generalize as course knowledge.
findings.fix: Reframe as what telomerase actually does vs the common textbook error, without the 'cross out the figure' framing.

findings.msg: DNA ligase is tagged `primer-removal`, but ligase seals the nick between Okazaki fragments — primer removal is Pol I's job, not ligase's.
findings.fix: Replace `primer-removal` with `leading-lagging` (keep `mechanism`).

findings.msg: Only one tag (`foundations`) — below the 2-tag minimum; same for L1-R002, L1-R004, and L1-C012 (all `foundations` alone).
findings.fix: Add a second content/skill tag (e.g. `compare-contrast` or `who-what-when`) to each.

findings.msg: Ligase again carries `primer-removal`; the card contrasts topoisomerase vs ligase, and ligase's role is nick-sealing, not primer removal.
findings.fix: Swap `primer-removal` for `leading-lagging`.

findings.msg: L1-C030 and L1-C013 both ask the mechanism for why smaller DNA migrates farther (sieve/pore matrix) — same answer.
findings.fix: Keep one.

findings.msg: 'Which step is in Southern but not Northern → restriction digestion' appears in L1-C009, L1-P007, and again inside L1-C020/C032 — 3-4x overlap.
findings.fix: Keep the problem (L1-P007) + one concept; trim the rest.

findings.msg: L2-R034 and L2-C010 both ask why terminal lagging-strand primer removal leaves an unreplicated end (no upstream 3'-OH) — same content.
findings.fix: Collapse to one.

findings.msg: States reverse transcriptase was discovered in 1971; Temin and Baltimore reported it in 1970 (Nature, June 1970).
findings.fix: Change 'Overturned in 1971' to '1970.'

findings.msg: Says LINEs + SINEs together are ~25% of the human genome; the accepted figure is closer to ~34% (LINEs ~20%, SINEs ~13%).
findings.fix: Update to '~33–34%.'

findings.msg: Oddly specific history cards (Astbury & Bell 1938 first X-ray photos; Carothers 1913 grasshoppers; Brock Yellowstone 1965) sit far from the molecular mechanisms an intro course tests.
findings.fix: Demote to optional or fold the key name/date into a mechanism card.

findings.msg: Herman Branson 'one-year sabbatical' biographical detail is trivia beyond intro-course scope.
findings.fix: Keep the α-helix/β-sheet + Pauling-Corey attribution; drop the sabbatical aside.

findings.msg: `telomere` is a stretch — the card is about reverse transcriptase and the updated central dogma; telomerase is only one example enzyme named.
findings.fix: Consider dropping `telomere`.

findings.msg: Card explicitly hinges on the method (X-ray crystallography + modeling) but omits the `technique` skill tag.
findings.fix: Add `technique`.

findings.msg: Sequence→peptide scanning cards (also L4-P015) rely on 5'→3' reading-frame logic but omit `directionality`.
findings.fix: Add `directionality`.

findings.msg: Telomere biology spans three overlapping batches in L2 (R018-24, C010-14, R034/35+C028-30); e.g. Hayflick/senescence in both C013 and C030, telomerase mechanism in C011 and R035.
findings.fix: Consolidate the third batch into the first two.

findings.msg: L4 carries ~18 wobble cards (R010-12, C023-29, P003/07/10/11/17-21); legal/illegal anticodon problems repeat the same logic many times — heavy even for a flagged high-yield topic.
findings.fix: Keep 3-4 canonical legal/illegal examples; trim redundant ones.

findings.msg: Gives B-DNA as ~10 bp/turn while L1-C024 gives ~10.5 bp/turn; harmless but inconsistent within the deck.
findings.fix: Standardize on 10.5 bp/turn (or note both are classic approximations).

findings.msg: L4–L7 (translation, mutation/repair, bacterial/viral genetics, gene regulation) extend well past the stated Unit 1 scope of 'DNA structure, replication, transcription' — fine for a full molgen course, but out of frame if this audit is Unit-1-only.
